title: How New GB300 NVL72 Features Provide Steady Power for AI

title: Designing Production-Ready Battery Energy Storage Systems for AI Factories

title: Item 6.2: NOGRR282 Large Electronic Load Ride-Through Requirements and NPRR1308 (Board memo)

title: NOGRR 282: ERCOT's Next Phase of Large Load Requirements
used_for: LEL = site with >=50% power-electronic computational load within Large Load category (>=75 MW aggregate); exemption cutoff 2025-11-14; enhanced requirements for newly energized LELs from 2028-01-01; filtered/time-delayed protection measurements

title: Large Load Integration (Batch Zero process)
used_for: Batch Zero interconnection process for loads >=75 MW; attestation forms; submission deadlines 2026-07-10 and 2026-07-24; PGRR145 framework

title: NERC Incident Review Examines Risks, Challenges Tied to Integration of Large Loads

title: NERC issues Level 3 alert, mandates action to address data center load losses

title: Taming Data Center Power Fluctuation With AI UPS
used_for: AI DC load swings '70 percent or more in milliseconds', 1 GW swings multiple times/minute; ON.energy bidirectional AI UPS, 3.5 MW units, 3 GW operating/under construction; Eaton+Microsoft developing lower-voltage bidirectional variants; ride-through support framing

title: Supercapacitors: Solving AI's Energy Spikes
used_for: Eaton XLHV supercap module 420 kW rack-size, 20-yr life; Siemens Energy E-statcom 75 MW, ms response; Delta Power Capacitance Shelf 15 kW/5 s; products already deployed at data centers

title: Neutralizing the Gigascale Problem: How to Solve the Physical Power Paradox of Extreme AI Training Loads
used_for: Millisecond-level pulse loads in 100 kW+ racks; UPS-integrated battery as the 'physical buffer'; ramp-rate control and 800 VDC/rack-BBU approaches

title: Why Jet Engines Could Power the AI Data Centers Boom

title: Energy demand from AI — Energy and AI
used_for: Data centres ~415 TWh (1.5% global) 2024 -> ~945 TWh 2030; US +~240 TWh (+130%), China +~175 TWh (+170%); accelerated servers ~half of growth

title: DOE Releases New Report Evaluating Increase in Electricity Demand from Data Centers (LBNL 2024)
